findings.problem: Silas and Inez fall back on the same assent/verdict pair so often in the reconciliation sequence that the exchange starts to feel templated rather than pressure-specific. Silas's repeated "All right" and Inez's repeated "Good" flatten some beats that should sound like distinct concessions, corrections, or recommitments.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:321-329, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:357-365, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:423-439, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:459-477, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:575-583, keep at most one of these repeated assent exchanges as a deliberate tic and rewrite the others so each reply registers the specific leverage of that moment: accountability, agreement to terms, erotic correction, or practical morning commitment.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if those spans no longer rely on the same bare assent/verdict beat three or more times and each revised reply makes clear what Silas is agreeing to or what Inez is ratifying in that exact moment; fail if the negotiation and aftermath still read as repeated 'All right'/'Good' placeholders.

findings.problem: Once the terms between Silas and Inez are already clear, the prose keeps reintroducing the contract/work symbolism inside the sex scene and immediate aftermath. The Ferrymouth board, jobs ledger, and explicit 'contractual' framing are strong anchors once, but repeated callbacks start annotating the chapter's meaning instead of trusting the bodies, consent language, and changed leverage to carry it.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:425-435 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:507-585, keep one decisive material callback to shared labor or the burned contract, then cut or redistribute the remaining board/ledger reminders so the scene can stay inside sensation, command, response, and post-coital consequence without re-stating the chapter thesis every few paragraphs.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised intimacy sequence uses no more than one explicit board/ledger/contract callback during the act itself and the thematic link between shared work and desire remains readable through action, consent, and aftermath alone; fail if multiple consecutive paragraphs in bed still stop to point back at the Ferrymouth board, jobs ledger, or contract logic.

summary: Chapter 17 clears its major structural obligations: Gideon's offer is genuinely tempting, the contract burn is materially decisive, Damaris turns private theft into public consequence, and the Inez sequence binds erotic recommitment to labor, money, consent, and unfinished trust without sanitizing any of it. Continuity, tense, and character-voice calibration all remain canon-safe. The fail is local rather than architectural: the back-half reconciliation scene slips into repeated assent beats and slightly over-explains its own work/sex/contract symbolism, which keeps the chapter just short of pass-level dialogue and prose control.
